headline: Illegal Logging Costs Sarawak RM2.8B in Lost Timber Revenue
context: The Sarawak Forestry Corporation estimated RM2.8 billion in timber revenue was lost to illegal logging operations in 2025, with satellite imagery revealing 48,000 hectares of protected forest cleared without valid permits.

cards.big: 48,000 hectares of Sarawak forest illegally cleared — RM2.8 billion in timber revenue stolen from the state and its people
cards.sub: Area cleared equals 67,000 football fields — visible from satellite but invisible to enforcement on the ground

cards.big: Legal timber industry contributes RM18 billion to Sarawak GDP but illegal operations capture an estimated 15% of total volume
cards.sub: Timber smuggling to Vietnam and China via transhipment costs Malaysia RM1.2 billion in annual export duties

cards.big: Indigenous communities in 14 districts report loss of traditional forest resources and clean water from illegal upstream logging
cards.sub: 120 Penan and Iban longhouses affected — customary land rights not recognised in Sarawak timber licensing framework

cards.big: Sarawak Forestry has 340 enforcement rangers covering 12.4 million hectares — 1 ranger per 36,470 hectares of forest
cards.sub: Only 18 prosecutions in 2025 despite satellite evidence of 280 illegal clearing incidents — enforcement gap is systemic

cards.big: Satellites see every illegally felled tree in real time — the question is why enforcement pretends it can't see what's visible from space
Should satellite-based forest monitoring trigger automatic enforcement action when clearing exceeds licensed boundaries?
